bonuses.leadin: This author wrote about depressed Slovenian woman in his 1998 novel Veronika Decides to Die. For 10 points each:
answers: Paulo Coelho [“ko-EY-lyoh”, though a phonetic English pronunciation is fine too]
an autobiography [or memoir; accept The Posthumous Memoirs of Bras Cubas or Memórias Póstumas de Brás Cubas]
parts: Name this contemporary novelist who wrote of an Andalusian author who journeys to Egypt in search of treasure in his 1988 novel The Alchemist.
Coelho wrote in this language, which his Brazilian countryman Jorge Amado used to write Tieta the Goat Girl and Gabriela, Clove and Cinnamon.

Fellow Brazilian author Joaquim Machado de Assis wrote a fictional work in this genre narrated by the deceased Bras Cubas. Mark Twain’s work in this genre was published in 2013, 103 years after his death.
answers_sanitized: Paulo Coelho ["ko-EY-lyoh", though a phonetic English pronunciation is fine too]
Portuguese language [or portugues; or lingua portuguesa]
an autobiography [or memoir; accept The Posthumous Memoirs of Bras Cubas or Memorias Postumas de Bras Cubas]
